The paragraph starts with "My final issue I would like to call to your attention is teacher's salaries." Then he says that high school students are America's future and that the future depends on the teachers. He mentions that teachers are working harder every day, while CEOs of big companies are getting bonuses, but he clarifies that he's not saying teachers' salaries should be the same as CEOs. However, he strongly believes action should be taken to increase teachers' salaries because they have one of the most important jobs in America, preparing the country for the future. Now, looking at the options: A) the teachers' salaries are not high enough. B) teacher's salaries should be as high as CEO's. C) the teachers work harder than CEO's of companies. Option C is tricky because he mentions teachers work harder, but it's in contrast to CEOs getting bonuses.
